fact: October 1 is exactly 274 days into the year, which means there are only 91 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, squirrels are typically frantically burying acorns and nuts around this time to prepare for winter!
The sun rises later and sets earlier than it did just a week ago - you're losing about 2-3 minutes of daylight every single day now in northern regions!
October 1 falls during Libra season, when the scales are balanced - just like how day and night were almost equal during the recent autumn equinox!
Maple trees in cold regions are usually showing off their brightest reds and oranges right now because they're shutting down their green chlorophyll for winter!

Monarch butterflies are often in the middle of their epic 3,000-mile journey south to Mexico around this time - some flying overhead have never made this trip before but somehow know exactly where to go!
The morning air feels crisp and smells different in autumn because plants have stopped growing and started breaking down their summer chemicals!
In the Southern Hemisphere, October 1 is like their version of April 1 - spring is exploding everywhere with baby animals and blooming flowers!
Spiders often appear everywhere in houses around this time because they're trying to escape the cooling outdoor temperatures!
The moon can look extra bright and orange when it rises in early October because you're seeing it through more of Earth's thick, dusty autumn atmosphere!
Geese flying overhead in their perfect V-formations can travel over 1,500 miles without stopping - and they're often migrating right around this date in North America!
October 1 is exactly 3 months away from New Year's Day!
